title: A heavy slab checkmark — but not a validation indicator. This mark is Precept's compile-time assertion: the entire class of invalid states has been made architecturally impossible before the first line of business logic runs. The tick doesn't confirm that something succeeded; it declares that failure was structurally foreclosed.
conceptualBridge: The compile-time check means Precept's prevention because the mark is not a runtime result but an architectural declaration that invalid states are structurally impossible at definition time.
decision: Exploring: avg 4.06, no hard fails — the compile-time reframing of the checkmark is conceptually strong, but the silhouette is heavily colonized by CI/CD tools (GitHub Actions, task managers) and doesn't help Precept establish a distinct visual identity; consider iterating to a more specific form.
